There are several free courses available online that offer training and certification for Certified Quality Auditor (CQA) exam. However, it is important to note that while some of these courses may be free, the certification exam itself will usually have a fee associated with it. Here are some options for free training to become a certified quality auditor:

1. Quality Council of Indiana: This online training course provides comprehensive training on all aspects of quality auditing, and includes practice quizzes and a sample test. While the course itself is free, there is a fee of $369 to take the certification exam.

2. ASQ: The American Society for Quality offers a free online course called "Introduction to Quality Auditing" that covers the fundamentals of auditing, including audit planning, conducting audits, and following up on findings. While this course does not provide a certification, it can be a good introduction to the field.

3. Udemy: This online learning platform offers several free courses on quality auditing, including "ISO 9001:2015 Internal Auditor Training" and "Quality Management Systems Auditor/Lead Auditor." While these courses do not provide a certification, they can be a good starting point for learning more about quality auditing.

4. Alison: This online learning platform offers a free course called "Diploma in Quality Management" which provides an in-depth look at quality management systems, including auditing and process improvement. While this course does not provide a certification, it can be a good way to gain knowledge and skills in the field.

The cost of the Certified Quality Auditor (CQA) course and exam fee varies based on the certification provider and your membership status with the organization. Here are the fees for some of the most popular CQA certification providers:

1. American Society for Quality (ASQ): The cost of the CQA exam for non-members starts at $438, while current ASQ members pay a discounted fee of $288. Additionally, all candidates pay a $70 application fee.

2. International Register of Certified Auditors (IRCA): The cost of the CQA training course and exam varies depending on the training organization you choose. It can cost anywhere from $1,500 to $3,500 for the training course and anywhere from $250 to $620 for the certification exam.

3. National Institute for Certification in Engineering Technologies (NICET): The NICET CQA exam fee starts at $450, with lower fees for NICET members, military personnel, and government employees.

It is important to keep in mind that these fees may be subject to change, and additional fees such as study materials, re-examination fees, and recertification fees may also apply.

In India, the cost of the Certified Quality Auditor (CQA) exam and training course varies based on the certification provider. Here are the approximate fees for some of the most popular CQA certification providers in India:

1. American Society for Quality (ASQ): The cost of the CQA exam for non-members is around INR 32,000, while current ASQ members pay a discounted fee of around INR 20,000. Additionally, all candidates pay an application fee of around INR 3,000.

2. International Register of Certified Auditors (IRCA): The cost of the CQA training course and exam varies depending on the training organization you choose. It can cost anywhere from INR 40,000 to INR 80,000 for the training course and anywhere from INR 4,000 to INR 8,000 for the certification exam.

3. National Institute for Certification in Engineering Technologies (NICET): The NICET CQA exam fee starts at around INR 25,000, with lower fees for NICET members, military personnel, and government employees.

It is important to keep in mind that these fees may be subject to change, and additional fees such as study materials, re-examination fees, and recertification fees may also apply.

IRCA (International Register of Certified Auditors) is a certification body that provides certification to quality professionals. In India, the salary of a quality professional with an IRCA certification varies depending on various factors such as qualification, experience, industry, and job role. Here are some approximate salary ranges for quality professionals with an IRCA certification:

1. Quality Assurance Manager: INR 5,00,000 - INR 10,00,000 per annum
2. Senior Quality Assurance Engineer: INR 4,00,000 - INR 8,00,000 per annum
3. Quality Control Manager: INR 4,50,000 - INR 8,50,000 per annum
4. Quality Control Analyst: INR 2,50,000 - INR 5,50,000 per annum
5. Quality Trainer: INR 3,00,000 - INR 6,00,000 per annum
6. Supplier Quality Engineer: INR 3,50,000 - INR 7,00,000 per annum 

It is important to keep in mind that these salary ranges are approximate and may vary based on the company, location, and other factors. Additionally, having an IRCA certification alone may not necessarily guarantee a higher salary, but it can demonstrate your expertise and enhance your career opportunities in the quality field.
